title: CBW Fentanyl Act
plain_summary: This bill gives the President new powers to punish foreign countries when their government workers help make or spread chemical weapons, biological weapons, or chemicals used to make fentanyl that harm other countries. The President must start with lighter punishments like stopping trade with that country's chemical companies, then move to stronger punishments like ending aid to that country, and finally blocking all money dealings with them if they don't fix the problem. The President can temporarily skip these punishments if it's really important for America's safety, and must stop the punishments when the foreign country fixes what they did wrong. The goal is to force countries to stop their governments from being involved in making dangerous chemicals and biological weapons that hurt people in other countries.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Countering Beijing's Weaponization of Fentanyl Act or the CBW Fentanyl Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Purposes
sections.text: This section changes the purposes listed in an existing law called the Chemical and Biological Weapons Control and Warfare Elimination Act from nineteen ninety one. It adds a new purpose to punish countries whose chemical or biological programs cause harm to other countries. The law will now aim to punish countries that use chemical weapons against their own people or other countries, use biological weapons against their own people or other countries, or commit acts involving chemical or biological programs that hurt or damage another country.

sections.title: Section 3 Presidential Reporting Requirements
sections.text: This section changes the reporting rules that the President must follow when dealing with chemical and biological weapons issues. It creates new rules for when the President gets reliable information that someone who works for a foreign government has committed a harmful act involving chemical or biological programs. When this happens, the President has sixty days to decide if the person really did commit the harmful act. If the President decides they did, the President must punish that person with sanctions. The President must consider several factors when making this decision, including physical evidence, whether the program has civilian or military uses, whether the foreign government tried to hide information from international organizations or other countries, whether they shared information voluntarily, and whether the country follows international treaties about chemical and biological weapons. The President must also report these decisions to Congress with specific details about any sanctions.

sections.title: Section 4 Required Sanctions on Foreign Countries in Response to Certain Acts Concerning Chemical or Biological Programs
sections.text: This section creates a new part of the existing law that requires the President to punish foreign countries when their government officials commit harmful acts involving chemical or biological programs. If the President determines that a government official has committed such an act, the President must impose three immediate punishments within thirty days. First, the United States must stop all scientific cooperation programs with that country. Second, the United States must ban the export of certain controlled items to that country. Third, the United States must stop buying goods or services from people working in the chemical or biological industries in that country. After one hundred twenty days, the President must report to Congress whether the foreign country has adequately addressed the harmful act, shared information with the United States and international organizations, and developed ways to prevent future harmful acts. The President must also report whether the country follows international treaties. If the country has not taken these actions, the President must impose at least two additional punishments from a list that includes stopping foreign aid except for urgent humanitarian help, banning more exports, and stopping weapons export licenses. After two hundred ten days, if the country still has not cooperated, the President must ban all financial transactions involving that country. The President can end these punishments after one year if the country addresses the harmful act, pays back people who were harmed, shares information, prevents future harmful acts, and follows international treaties. The President can temporarily waive these punishments for up to one hundred eighty days if it is vital to United States national security, but this waiver power ends five years after this law takes effect.

sections.title: Section 311 Definitions
sections.text: This section defines important terms used in the law. A chemical or biological program means a program to make, develop, or distribute chemical or biological weapons or specific fentanyl-related chemicals like benzylfentanyl, four anilinopiperidine, or norfentanyl precursors. The Commerce Control List is a list of controlled items maintained by the Department of Commerce. A covered act means something done by a government official who knew or should have known their action would hurt another country, and the action involves a chemical or biological program controlled by their government. Covered treaties include the Biological Weapons Convention from nineteen seventy two and the Chemical Weapons Convention from nineteen ninety two. Export Administration Regulations are rules about controlling exports found in federal regulations. A foreign governmental entity includes foreign governments, their agencies, entities they control, entities that act for them, and entities that receive significant support from them and engage in commercial activities like shipping or manufacturing.

sections.title: Section 5 Conforming Amendment
sections.text: This section makes a small change to another part of the existing law to make sure the references match the new changes made by this bill.
